publication_scope: govern public route names before deployment
govern public route stability and status vocabulary
govern public data files and future schema expectations
govern static artifact and future generated-artifact separation
govern base-path and domain portability expectations
govern future custom-domain and alternate-static-host readiness without configuring hosts
govern future static-to-live backend handoff routes without implementing a hosted backend
govern future live source probe policy without implementing probes or network calls
govern future public search request, response, error, and route envelopes before implementing public search runtime
govern compatibility-surface projections without implementing snapshots, relay, native apps, or new runtime behavior
govern future local relay surface design, security/privacy defaults, operator controls, and protocol candidates without implementing relay runtime
govern public claim traceability before public hosting
govern signed/offline snapshot format, checksums, and signature-placeholder policy without real signing keys
govern seed snapshot example separately from the current site/dist deployment artifact

govern future native action, download, install handoff, package-manager handoff, mirror, execute, restore, uninstall, rollback, malware-scan, and rights-warning policy without implementing those actions

govern future native/local cache, privacy, private data, local path, telemetry, logging, credential, deletion, export, portable-mode, and relay privacy policy without implementing cache runtime or private ingestion

public_claim_source_policy.rule: No public claim without a repo source.

public_status_taxonomy.meaning: The referenced artifact or route exists in the current repo artifact.
The claim or data comes from committed recorded fixtures, not live systems.
The route or file is safe static demonstration material, not live backend behavior.
The item is named for planning but does not implement behavior.
The item is accepted as future work but is not implemented.
The item is intentionally out of the current milestone.
The item depends on an unmet prerequisite.
The item is not safe to expose in the current public-alpha posture.
The item was removed from the public contract.

route_stability_levels.meaning: May change while publication contracts are still pre-alpha.
Intended to persist, but still subject to review before stable status.
Durable public route or data contract; changes require deprecation.
Still present but scheduled for removal or replacement.
No longer present in the active public contract.

next_expected_milestones: Public Search Result Card Contract v0
Public Search Safety / Abuse Guard v0
Local Public Search Runtime v0 only after contract and safety review
Rust Local Index Parity Candidate v0 only after review and Cargo availability
Relay Prototype Implementation v0 only after explicit human approval
Windows 7 WinForms Native Skeleton Implementation v0 only after explicit human approval
Internet Archive Live Probe v0 only after explicit human approval and live probe gateway review
notes: This contract supersedes the previous immediate Deployment Config Pack ordering.
GitHub Pages deployment mechanics consume these contracts rather than define public architecture.
The current static artifact remains site/dist and is not renamed by this slice.
Repository Shape Consolidation v0 makes site/dist the single generated static deployment artifact.
Generated Public Data Summaries v0 adds static machine-readable summaries under site/dist/data.
Lite/Text/Files Seed Surfaces v0 adds static compatibility seed surfaces under site/dist/lite, site/dist/text, and site/dist/files.
Static Resolver Demo Snapshots v0 adds fixture-backed static resolver demo pages under site/dist/demo.
Custom Domain / Alternate Host Readiness v0 adds domain/static-host readiness inventories, base-path portability docs, and validation without DNS, CNAME, alternate-host config, or backend deployment.
Live Backend Handoff Contract v0 reserves /api/v1 route families, capability flags, and error-envelope expectations without deploying a backend or making /api/v1 live.
Live Probe Gateway Contract v0 records disabled-by-default live probe source policy, limits, cache/evidence requirements, and operator gates without implementing probes or making network calls.

Public Search API Contract v0 defines future local_index_only public search request, response, error, and route envelopes without implementing /search, /api/v1/search, backend hosting, live probes, downloads, installs, uploads, local path search, arbitrary URL fetching, or production API stability.
Rust Query Planner Parity Candidate v0 adds an isolated Rust planner candidate against Python-oracle goldens without replacing Python or wiring Rust into runtime surfaces.
Compatibility Surface Strategy v0 records surface capability and route matrices plus old-client/native/snapshot/relay readiness policy without implementing new runtime behavior.
No provider backend configuration, live backend, or live source probe is added by this contract.

Signed Snapshot Format v0 adds a snapshot contract, checksum/signature-placeholder policy, and deterministic repo seed example under snapshots/examples/static_snapshot_v0 without adding real signing keys, executable downloads, production signatures, relay behavior, native clients, or a public /snapshots/ route.
The public /snapshots/ route remains future/deferred; site/dist remains the current static deployment artifact.

Relay Surface Design v0 adds a future local/LAN relay design inventory, reference docs, security/privacy defaults, and unsigned operator checklist without implementing a relay server, opening sockets, adding FTP/SMB/WebDAV/Gopher behavior, exposing private data, or enabling live probes.

Relay Prototype Planning v0 selects a future localhost-only read-only static local HTTP relay prototype over allowlisted public data and seed snapshot files without implementing a relay server, opening sockets, adding protocol behavior, serving private files, proxying a live backend, enabling live probes, or claiming old-client relay support.

Native Action / Download / Install Policy v0 adds policy-only action classes, warning classes, public-alpha/static defaults, and native/snapshot/relay gates for future download, mirror, install handoff, package-manager handoff, execute, restore, uninstall, rollback, malware-scan, and rights/access behavior without implementing downloads, installers, package-manager integration, malware scanning, rights clearance, native clients, relay runtime, or executable trust claims.

Native Local Cache / Privacy Policy v0 adds policy-only privacy/cache defaults, prohibited private-data behaviors, telemetry/logging/credential rules, and native/snapshot/relay/public-alpha gates without implementing cache runtime, private file ingestion, local archive scanning, telemetry, accounts, cloud sync, uploads, native clients, or relay runtime.
